Description: The case scenario to ask the candidate.

Additional info to be be provided if candidate asks. The list is used to represent a case with multiple prompts and additional info for each prompt. The length of the list == number of prompts for case.
If it's a interview led question, there's usually multiple follow-up questions after the main case scenario is given. The length of the list == number of follow-up questions for the case.
Link to images/screenshots of the exhibits for the case. This is also a list as there may be multiple exhibits in a case.
The rubric to grade the performance on the case
The company that asks the case question
Values: [mckinsey, bcg, bain, deloitte, accenture, kearney, oliver-wyman, roland-berger, lek, strategy-and]

Description: The core thing the case is evaluating/testing
Values: [profitability, market-entry, market-sizing, m&a, operations, growth-strategy, pricing, competitive-response, brainteasers, turnarounds, strategic-decision, industry-analysis]

Description: The difficulty of the case question
What industry is the case about
Values: [consumer-packaged-goods, oil&gas, manufacturing, financial-services, healthcare, private-equity, Investments, pharmaceuticals, airline, media, technology]

Description: The casebook (e.g. Tuck2009) the question came from
